findings.detail: 7-node flowchart TB. Three dimension nodes (SEV, PROB, DET) each fire an edge into COST, expressing the fan-in 'three dimensions feeding into a combined cost signal' as the intent specifies. COST then feeds VERDICT (a diamond), which branches via labelled edges 'High cost' → VERIFY and 'Low cost' → PROCEED — exactly the triage decision flow described in §5 and §6.

All seven bold labels trace directly to corpus §5–§6: 'Assess Severity' (§5 Dimension 1 / §6 Step 2), 'Assess Probability' (§5 Dimension 2 / §6 Step 3), 'Assess Detectability' (§5 Dimension 3 / §6 Step 4), 'Combined Cost Signal' (§5 formula Severity × Probability × 1/Detectability), 'Cost Verdict' (§5 high/low cost triage decision), 'Verify Before Acting' (§6 Step 5 high-cost path), 'Proceed with Care' (§6 Step 5 low-cost path). No Q2 or Q3 nodes present. No concept drift.

All bold node labels ≤ 4 words: 'Assess Severity' 2w, 'Assess Probability' 2w, 'Assess Detectability' 2w, 'Combined Cost Signal' 3w, 'Cost Verdict' 2w, 'Verify Before Acting' 3w, 'Proceed with Care' 3w. Pass.

SEV, PROB, DET, COST all use auto purple (#d0bfff/#8b5cf6) — they are the automated/systematic triage assessment steps. VERDICT uses decision/hitl yellow (#fff3bf/#f59e0b, stroke-width 3px) — it is the human judgment gate where the cost verdict is rendered. VERIFY and PROCEED use done green (#b2f2bb/#22c55e) — they are terminal action outcomes. Semantically coherent.
Governing concept 'Q1 — What is the Cost of This Being Wrong?' sits in the --- config title: field as a banner above the diagram. No ghost node used for the central organizing idea.

TB is correct for this intent. The relationship is genuinely non-linear: three dimension nodes converge (fan-in) to COST, then COST fans-out to two terminal branches. A TB layout expresses this naturally — dimensions align horizontally at the top rank, COST sits in the middle, VERDICT and the two terminal nodes fill the bottom ranks. An LR layout would force this fan-in/fan-out into an awkward left-right expansion.

All subtitle spans use font-size 12px (above the 11px floor), color #6d28d9. Content traces to corpus: 'How bad if the error occurs?' (§5 Dimension 1), 'How likely is this output wrong?' (§5 Dimension 2 / §5 triage checklist Q2), 'Would you catch it before harm?' (§5 Dimension 3 / §5 triage checklist Q3), 'Severity × Probability × (1 / Detectability)' (§5 formula), 'Apply deliberate scrutiny' (§6 Step 5), 'Move faster, stay alert' (§5 'move faster' phrasing). All grounded.

preflight.mermaid_parse: pass (valid --- YAML config block with config: wrapper; flowchart TB declaration; SEV/PROB/DET/COST in bracket notation with HTML labels; VERDICT in brace notation for diamond; VERIFY/PROCEED in bracket notation; all edges use --> and -->|"label"| syntax; classDef declarations for auto/start/done/decision; class assignments resolve to defined classDefs; no stray tokens)
preflight.node_count: pass (7 nodes: SEV, PROB, DET, COST, VERDICT, VERIFY, PROCEED — within 3–8 range)
preflight.label_discipline: pass (all bold labels ≤ 4 words — longest is 'Combined Cost Signal' at 3 words)
preflight.concept_fidelity: pass (all labels trace to corpus §5 Core Concepts and §6 Implementation; no Q2/Q3 or adjacent-topic scope creep; 'Combined Cost Signal' maps to the § 5 formula node)
preflight.jargon_labels: pass (no bare unexplained acronyms; all labels plain English or corpus-defined terms)
preflight.title_discipline: pass (governing concept in config title: field; no ghost node displacement)
preflight.linear_flow_discipline: pass (intent is explicitly non-linear: three-to-one fan-in + one-to-two branch; TB layout with convergence/divergence is appropriate; no spurious branching on a genuinely linear concept)
